title: Flaky Nutty Venezuelan Biscuit
description: A nutty, flaky, energizing Venezuelan bread featuring orange blossom water and a double-rise structure, suitable for Mediterranean diets.

ingredients.name: all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: Use a high-protein flour for better rise.

ingredients.name: whole wheat flour
ingredients.notes: Adds nutty flavor and extra fiber.

ingredients.name: yeast
ingredients.notes: Active dry yeast for a double-rise effect.

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: Regulates yeast growth and enhances flavor.

ingredients.name: large eggs
ingredients.notes: One egg for egg wash and richness.

ingredients.name: warm milk
ingredients.notes: For yeast activation and dough development.

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine all-purpose flour, whole wheat flour, yeast, and salt.
Whisk dry ingredients to combine, then create a well in the center for the wet ingredients.
In a separate bowl, whisk together orange blossom water, sugar, egg, and melted butter.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and gradually mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Gradually add warm milk, then water, until the dough comes together in a sticky ball.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 10 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Form the dough into a ball and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, until doubled in size.
Punch down the dough and shape it into a round or oblong loaf.
Place the loaf on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, and let it rise for another 30 minutes.
Brush the egg wash over the loaf and sprinkle with sesame seeds or poppy seeds if using.
Bake the loaf for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown and cooked through.
Remove the loaf from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes before slicing.
